name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow, Inc. is the leading enterprise AI workflow platform, orchestrating 80 billion+ enterprise workflows annually across IT, HR, customer service, security, and finance. The Now Platform connects systems, data, and processes with AI agents to eliminate friction in enterprise operations. ServiceNow serves over 8,100 customers including 85% of the Fortune 500, with subscription revenue of $3.67B in Q1 2026 (22% YoY growth). CEO Bill McDermott positions the company as the 'Operating System for AI' in the enterprise.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow has successfully transformed from an ITSM ticketing system (2004-2015) to an enterprise platform (2015-2020) to the defining enterprise AI workflow platform (2020-present) — a multi-stage business model transformation that expanded TAM from ~$20B to $220B+ [1][2]. Each pivot built on the previous platform while fundamentally redefining the category. The Moveworks acquisition added AI customer service, and the Armis acquisition (closing H2 2026) adds cybersecurity. AI Deployment (2/2): Now Assist AI exceeded $600M in ACV and is tracking toward $1B in 2026, with net new ACV from AI products growing at triple-digit rates in Q1 2026 [1]. AI agents are shipping to customers with specific monetization through per-skill AI SKUs (Pro+, Enterprise+). AI Governance features launched to ensure enterprise AI agents operate within ethical and legal boundaries. Market Share (2/2): ServiceNow is the undisputed category leader in enterprise workflow orchestration, with 630 customers exceeding $5M ACV (up 22% YoY) and 16 transactions over $5M net new ACV in Q1 2026 (nearly 80% YoY growth) [1]. No close independent competitor exists at this scale in enterprise workflow. Product Pipeline (1/2): Armis cybersecurity acquisition closing H2 2026, agentic AI enterprise features, and AI Governance product represent named products with general timelines but limited specific revenue visibility for new categories. R&D Intensity (1/1): ServiceNow invests heavily in platform R&D, with the Now Platform representing decades of engineering investment in workflow orchestration that creates deep competitive moats.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's mission to 'make the world of work, work better for people' is specific, service-oriented, and connected to a genuine societal benefit — removing friction from enterprise workflows to free humans for higher-value work [2]. McDermott's framing of ServiceNow as the 'Operating System for AI' that 'integrates with any model, cloud, interface, data, and system' reflects a thoughtful purpose that is validated by consistent platform expansion. The purpose is clearly referenced in strategic decisions and product development. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow.org provides platform access to nonprofits, and the company has committed to carbon neutrality. These are genuine but not distinctive relative to ServiceNow's scale and profitability [2]. Employee Retention (1/2): ServiceNow employs 29,187 people and has built a culture that the Board describes as having an 'important connection between culture and performance' [2]. McDermott's employee-centric leadership style and the 'human-AI' narrative framing AI as amplifying employees rather than replacing them creates organizational alignment. However, specific retention metrics are not publicly available. Insider Buying (2/2): CEO McDermott purchased $3M of NOW shares on the open market during the January 2026 software sector selloff and extended his contract to 2030, demonstrating meaningful personal financial conviction at a moment of market stress [2].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. ServiceNow's financial infrastructure is near-exemplary: Q1 2026 subscription revenues of $3.67B (22% YoY growth) with raised FY2026 guidance to $15.74-15.78B (20.5-21% growth) [1]. D/E of 20.7% is pristine for an enterprise software company, with $5.2B in cash against $2.4B in debt. ROE of 16.1% exceeds the 15% target. Revenue growth of 22.1% YoY and estimated 5-year CAGR of 24.1% both significantly exceed targets. Forward P/E of 22.7 is reasonable for a 20%+ growth compounder, nearly at the 20 target. Profit margin of 12.6% is the primary weakness, below the 20% target due to heavy R&D and sales investment, though non-GAAP operating margin is 32%. PEG ratio of 1.09 is near the 1.0 target. Free cash flow of $5.1B demonstrates strong cash conversion on $14B TTM revenue [4].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (5/9): ServiceNow occupies a unique position in the AI landscape — CEO McDermott argues that 'AI excels at identifying problems and providing advice but cannot execute solutions,' positioning ServiceNow as the essential execution layer that AI agents require to function within enterprises [2]. The platform orchestrates 80B+ enterprise workflows annually and integrates deeply with legacy IT infrastructure, creating high switching costs. However, the risk is real and non-trivial: agentic AI systems from Microsoft (Copilot Studio), Salesforce (Agentforce), and startups could mature to bypass ServiceNow's workflow layer entirely, handling both intelligence and execution. The Q1 2026 stock decline from $211 to $114 (down 46% from 52-week high) partially reflects market anxiety that AI could disrupt rather than empower enterprise workflow software. Analysts are split — JPMorgan flags ServiceNow as 'AI-resilient' while bears argue agentic AI could collapse the workflow orchestration layer within 3-5 years. AI Opportunity (1/1): Now Assist AI exceeded $600M in ACV tracking toward $1B in 2026, with triple-digit growth in AI net new ACV [1]. This is meaningful, quantified AI revenue that demonstrates ServiceNow is monetizing AI directly.

infinite_game.summary: ServiceNow under Bill McDermott demonstrates many characteristics of an infinite game player — a genuine purpose that shapes product decisions, transformative platform pivots, and a CEO willing to put personal capital at risk during market stress. However, the company's growth-at-premium-valuation model, heavy stock-based compensation, and the enterprise software industry's inherent focus on quarterly subscription metrics create tension with pure infinite game thinking. McDermott is a visionary operator, but ServiceNow's strategic decisions are heavily optimized for maintaining 20%+ subscription growth — a finite metric.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): ServiceNow's cause — making work work better for people by eliminating enterprise friction — is genuine, service-oriented, and specific [2]. It describes a future state (frictionless enterprise workflows) that doesn't fully exist and invites customers and partners to contribute. McDermott's consistent articulation of this cause across multiple economic cycles validates its authenticity. The evolution to 'Operating System for AI' extends the purpose naturally. However, the cause has primarily been validated under favorable market conditions and has not been tested through a true existential crisis for the company. Display score: 7.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): ServiceNow employs 29,187 people and the Board explicitly describes 'the important connection between culture and performance' [2]. McDermott's employee-centric leadership style and the 'human-AI' framing create organizational alignment. The company invests in employee AI training and positions AI as amplifying rather than replacing workers. However, enterprise software companies routinely have significant voluntary attrition, and ServiceNow's rapid headcount growth (from ~26K to 29K+) dilutes cultural cohesion. No specific tenure or turnover data publicly available. Display score: 6.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): McDermott publicly acknowledges Salesforce, SAP, and Microsoft as worthy rivals whose capabilities push ServiceNow to develop better platform integrations and expand use cases [2]. The Moveworks and Armis acquisitions reflect competitive awareness — rivals' strengths revealing ServiceNow's portfolio gaps in AI customer service and cybersecurity. The company frames competition as motivation to strengthen the platform rather than zero-sum warfare. Display score: 10.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): The three-stage transformation from ITSM ticketing system (2004-2015) to enterprise platform (2015-2020) to enterprise AI platform (2020-present) represents textbook existential flexibility [1][2]. Each pivot expanded TAM by an order of magnitude while building on the existing platform's strengths. ServiceNow didn't defend its ITSM monopoly — it redefined the category. The current repositioning as 'Operating System for AI' is another transformative bet in real time. Display score: 10.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): McDermott's $3M personal share purchase during the January 2026 software sector selloff — when the stock dropped significantly on AI disruption fears — demonstrates personal financial courage in service of the company's direction [2]. His contract extension to 2030 signals long-term commitment. However, ServiceNow's strategic bets (AI platform, Armis acquisition) are funded from a position of extreme strength ($5.2B cash, $5.1B FCF) rather than requiring genuine sacrifice. The Rule of 55+ standard maintains discipline but is also consensus best practice for premium software companies. Display score: 6.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the enterprise AI platform that benefits from the AI boom as the execution layer that AI agents require to function. Q1 2026 delivered $3.67B subscription revenue (22% YoY growth), 16 deals over $5M net new ACV (nearly 80% YoY growth), and raised FY2026 subscription guidance to $15.74-15.78B [1]. Now Assist AI exceeded $600M ACV tracking toward $1B, with triple-digit AI net new ACV growth demonstrating the platform is successfully monetizing AI. At $114.19, the stock has corrected 46% from its $211 52-week high, compressing the forward P/E from 52x to 22.7x — creating a potentially attractive entry for a 20%+ growth compounder with $5.1B free cash flow, pristine D/E of 20.7%, and PEG of 1.09. CEO McDermott's $3M open-market share purchase and 2030 contract extension signal maximum long-term conviction [2].
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on whether agentic AI will empower or replace enterprise workflow orchestration. The 46% stock decline from highs reflects genuine market uncertainty about this question — if Microsoft Copilot Studio, Salesforce Agentforce, or AI-native startups mature to handle both intelligence and execution, ServiceNow's orchestration layer could become redundant within 3-5 years [1]. The trailing P/E of 68x and GAAP profit margin of only 12.6% demonstrate that the stock prices in substantial future earnings growth, and any deceleration in the 20%+ subscription growth rate would trigger severe multiple compression. The Armis acquisition adds integration risk and dilution at a time of elevated uncertainty. Stock-based compensation remains heavy for an enterprise software company of this maturity, diluting per-share economics. The stock's 46% drawdown may not be complete if the AI disruption thesis gains further traction.
key_catalysts: Now Assist AI reaching $1B ACV in 2026 would validate the 'Operating System for AI' thesis and demonstrate that AI is additive rather than competitive to platform revenues
Armis cybersecurity acquisition closing H2 2026, expanding platform TAM and demonstrating successful M&A integration alongside the Moveworks acquisition
FY2026 subscription revenue of $15.74-15.78B (20.5-21% growth) maintained or exceeded, proving that the business can sustain premium growth rates despite macro and competitive headwinds
key_risks: Agentic AI platforms from Microsoft, Salesforce, and startups maturing to handle both intelligence and execution, potentially collapsing the enterprise workflow orchestration layer that is ServiceNow's core value proposition
46% stock decline from $211 high may not be complete — trailing P/E of 68x and 12.6% GAAP margin leave minimal margin of safety if subscription growth decelerates below 20%
Heavy stock-based compensation and Armis acquisition integration complexity create execution risk and per-share dilution at a time of elevated market uncertainty about the AI thesis
sources: ServiceNow Q1 FY2026 Financial Results and Raised Subscription Guidance — SEC Form 8-K, April 2026
ServiceNow CEO Bill McDermott Leadership, AI Strategy, and $3M Share Purchase — BBNTIMES, BYDFi, SEC Proxy 2026
ServiceNow Insider Trading and Employee Retention Programs — SEC Form S-8, DEF 14A, 2026
Yahoo Finance — NOW Key Statistics and Financial Data, accessed June 2026
